channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: These Paralympians inspire their workplace colleagues to pursue richer lives outside the office
item.description: If you walked into Oyuna Uranchimeg ’s office at the University of St. Thomas’ emerging media department, you’d see a poster from the Beijing 2022 Oly...

item.title: Lela Rose finally gets her boots
item.description: While some girls dream of getting their first designer handbag, Lela Rose—who grew up in Dallas—dreamt of getting her own pair of boots from Lucchese,...

item.title: Want to Instantly Perform Better in Job Interviews? Try This FBI Hostage Negotiation Technique
item.description: A little mirroring can turn what would be a question-and-answer session into a real conversation.

item.title: OpenAI just dragged its own brand
item.description: It sounds like a brag-worthy business coup: not just snagging a high-profile client, but doing so just after your chief rival’s deal with that same cl...

item.title: The Most Underrated Emotional Intelligence Skill? Learning to Laugh at Yourself
item.description: Research backs up this assertion from best-selling author Adam Grant: ‘Taking your responsibilities seriously but your ego lightly is a core life skil...

item.title: $3.7 billion whisper: the explosive growth of quiet corporate activism
item.description: Sometimes, silence speaks volumes. There's a quiet revolution taking place in corporate purpose.

item.title: We’re economists who designed a chatbot to help our students reason instead of cheat. Meet ‘Macro Buddy’
item.description: About 90% of 1,100 U.S. students surveyed at two-year and four-year colleges in 2025 reported using generative AI.

item.title: McDonald’s CEO Posted a Cringe Video. Here’s the Astonishing Lesson You Can Learn From It
item.description: Chris Kempczinski made a widely-mocked video. Maybe that’s a good thing.

item.title: “Technology is our superpower”—Africa and India take seat at top table as AI revolution spreads
item.description: Emerging economies like Nigeria and India are leapfrogging into a tech-driven future, where mobile networks and AI could redefine development—and resh...

item.title: This Block Employee Survived the ‘Thanos Snap’—Then Refused a 90 Percent Pay Bump and Quit Immediately. Why Her Explanation Is Going Viral
item.description: The data scientist says the company ‘shoved AI down everyone’s throats,’ yet she saw ‘very limited gains in productivity.’

item.title: Grocery Outlet is closing stores, joins growing list of retail chains shuttering locations in 2026
item.description: Grocery Outlet is joining the ranks of retailers planning to shutter storefronts this year. The discount grocery store chain announced its fourth-quar...

item.title: Side Hustles Are Booming. Here Are 5 Tips for Finding the Right Gig for You
item.description: There’s never been a better time to turn a passion into a money-making venture.

item.title: The last 3 years were the hottest ever recorded. Here’s why we may look back at them as some of the coolest we remember
item.description: By most indicators, the planet should have been cooler in 2025 than it was.

item.title: Your employees aren’t disengaged. They’ve got screen fatigue
item.description: For the past few years, leaders have been trying to decode what’s happening to attention at work. We’ve debated burnout, quiet quitting, and whether y...

item.title: The President is surprised by the lack of European support for military action against Iran—he shouldn’t be
item.description: With Iraq’s legacy still lingering and a changing America in view, Europe is no longer willing to fall in line behind Washington’s wars—even when alli...
